content.s1_claims_discipline_internal:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: nutrition protocols, therapeutic diets, food plans, meal timing, anti-inflammatory protocol, elimination protocol, gut healing diet, keto protocol, carnivore protocol, AIP protocol, low histamine, low FODMAP, low oxalate, Mediterranean protocol, Wahls protocol, specific carbohydrate diet, SCD, GAPS diet, diet plan, food protocol, what to eat, meal plan, anti-cancer diet, diabetes diet, heart healthy diet, weight loss diet, gut healing food, autoimmune diet, histamine intolerance food, calorie restriction, fasting mimicking, Valter Longo, therapeutic nutrition, food as medicine, meal plans, eating plans, condition-specific diet, therapeutic diet, anti-inflammatory meal plan, metabolic protocol nutrition, reintroduction, food diary, macros, calorie needs, eating schedule, pre-post workout nutrition
# CATEGORY: Modalities
# USE FOR: Specific nutrition protocols, meal plans, condition-specific diets, therapeutic nutrition
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 04_NUTRITION_DIET, 12_GUT, 22_AUTOIMMUNE, 06_METABOLIC, 29_WEIGHT_BODY_COMP, 25_ONCOLOGY_SUPPORT
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# THERAPEUTIC NUTRITION PROTOCOLS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## NUTRITION FUNDAMENTALS

**No single diet is right for everyone.**

Our approach:
- Individualized to your needs, goals, and genetics
- Whole foods over processed
- Blood sugar stability
- Adequate protein
- Healthy fats
- Abundant vegetables
- Mindful eating

**The best diet is one you can sustain that makes you feel good.**

---

## CORE PRINCIPLES

### 1. Whole Foods First

**Eat foods your great-grandmother would recognize.**

| Emphasize | Minimize |
|-----------|----------|
| Vegetables | Processed foods |
| Fruits | Refined sugars |
| Quality proteins | Seed oils |
| Healthy fats | Artificial ingredients |
| Whole grains (if tolerated) | Packaged snacks |
| Legumes (if tolerated) | Fast food |

### 2. Blood Sugar Stability

**Stable blood sugar = Stable energy, mood, and weight.**

| Strategy | How |
|----------|-----|
| Protein at every meal | Slows glucose absorption |
| Healthy fats | Slows glucose absorption |
| Fiber | Slows glucose absorption |
| Limit refined carbs | Prevents spikes |
| Don't drink calories | Juice, soda, sweet drinks |
| Vinegar before meals | May reduce glucose response |

### 3. Adequate Protein

**Most people undereat protein.**

| Goal | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Minimum | 0.8 g/kg body weight |
| Active/Muscle building | 1.2-1.6 g/kg |
| GLP-1 medications | 1.6-2.0 g/kg |
| Older adults | 1.2-1.5 g/kg |

**30-40g protein per meal is a good target for most.**

### 4. Healthy Fats

| Include | Limit/Avoid |
|---------|-------------|
| Olive oil | Seed oils (canola, corn, soy, sunflower) |
| Avocado | Trans fats |
| Nuts and seeds | Margarine |
| Fatty fish | Fried foods |
| Coconut oil | Highly processed fats |
| Grass-fed butter | |

### 5. Fiber

**Target: 25-35g daily (most get <15g)**

**Sources:**
- Vegetables (all)
- Fruits (with skin)
- Legumes
- Nuts and seeds
- Whole grains (if tolerated)

---

## THERAPEUTIC DIETS

### Mediterranean Diet

**The most researched, broadly beneficial diet.**

**Core components:**
- Abundant vegetables and fruits
- Olive oil as primary fat
- Fish 2-3x weekly
- Legumes regularly
- Nuts and seeds
- Moderate whole grains
- Limited red meat
- Limited dairy (yogurt, cheese)
- Moderate red wine (optional)

**Benefits:**
- Cardiovascular health
- Cognitive function
- Longevity
- Anti-inflammatory
- Blood sugar regulation

**Best for:** Most people, cardiovascular risk, general health

### Low-Carbohydrate Diet

**Reduces carbohydrates to varying degrees.**

| Type | Carb Target | Notes |
|------|-------------|-------|
| Moderate low-carb | 50-100g/day | Sustainable long-term |
| Low-carb | 20-50g/day | More aggressive |
| Ketogenic | <20g/day | Therapeutic ketosis |

**Benefits:**
- Insulin resistance/diabetes
- Weight loss
- Metabolic syndrome
- Neurological conditions (seizures, may help others)
- Mental clarity (once adapted)

**Challenges:**
- Initial adaptation ("keto flu")
- Social situations
- May not be sustainable for everyone
- Requires planning

**Best for:** Insulin resistance, metabolic syndrome, weight loss, Type 2 diabetes

### Ketogenic Diet

**Very low carb, high fat to achieve ketosis.**

**Macronutrients:**
- Carbs: <20-50g (5-10% of calories)
- Protein: Moderate (15-20%)
- Fat: High (70-80%)

**Ketone targets:**
- Nutritional ketosis: 0.5-3.0 mmol/L
- Therapeutic (oncology): 2-5 mmol/L

**Benefits:**
- Significant for epilepsy
- Neurological conditions
- Insulin resistance
- May benefit some cancers (see 25_ONCOLOGY)
- Cognitive enhancement

**Requires:** Careful implementation, electrolyte management, monitoring

### Anti-Inflammatory Diet

**Reduces inflammation through food choices.**

**Include:**
- Fatty fish (omega-3)
- Olive oil
- Colorful vegetables
- Berries
- Nuts
- Turmeric, ginger
- Green tea
- Dark leafy greens

**Avoid:**
- Sugar and refined carbs
- Seed oils
- Processed foods
- Excessive alcohol
- Known personal triggers

**Best for:** Autoimmune, chronic inflammation, joint issues, general health

### AIP Diet (Autoimmune Protocol)

**Elimination diet for autoimmune conditions.**

**Eliminate:**
- Grains (all)
- Legumes
- Dairy
- Eggs
- Nuts and seeds
- Nightshades (tomatoes, peppers, potatoes, eggplant)
- Alcohol
- Coffee
- Sugar
- NSAIDs

**Emphasize:**
- Quality meats, fish, organ meats
- Vegetables (non-nightshade)
- Fruits (limited)
- Healthy fats
- Bone broth
- Fermented foods (if tolerated)

**Duration:** 30-90 days elimination, then systematic reintroduction

**See 22_AUTOIMMUNE for full protocol.**

### Elimination Diet

**Gold standard for identifying food sensitivities.**

**Process:**
1. **Eliminate** common triggers for 3-4 weeks
2. **Reintroduce** one food every 3-7 days
3. **Monitor** symptoms carefully
4. **Identify** your personal triggers

**Common triggers:** Gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, corn, nuts, nightshades

### Low-FODMAP Diet

**For IBS and SIBO.**

**FODMAPs:** Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides, and Polyols

**High-FODMAP (avoid initially):**
- Garlic, onion
- Wheat
- Lactose (milk)
- Excess fructose (apples, honey)
- Stone fruits
- Legumes
- Certain vegetables (cauliflower, mushrooms)

**Process:**
1. Strict elimination (2-6 weeks)
2. Systematic reintroduction
3. Personalize based on tolerance

**Best for:** IBS, SIBO (during treatment), bloating

### Low-Histamine Diet

**For histamine intolerance or MCAS.**

**High-histamine foods to avoid:**
- Aged cheeses
- Fermented foods
- Alcohol (especially red wine)
- Cured meats
- Smoked fish
- Vinegar
- Leftover proteins
- Certain vegetables (tomatoes, spinach, eggplant)
- Certain fruits (citrus, strawberries)

**Histamine liberators to avoid:**
- Citrus
- Chocolate
- Nuts
- Shellfish
- Tomatoes

**Best for:** MCAS, histamine intolerance, chronic urticaria

---

## PROTEIN GUIDE

### Protein Sources

| Source | Protein per Serving | Notes |
|--------|-------------------|-------|
| Chicken breast (4oz) | 26g | Lean, versatile |
| Salmon (4oz) | 25g | Omega-3 |
| Eggs (2 large) | 12g | Complete protein |
| Greek yogurt (1 cup) | 15-20g | Probiotics |
| Cottage cheese (1 cup) | 25g | Casein (slow) |
| Beef (4oz) | 28g | Iron, B12 |
| Tofu (4oz) | 10g | Plant-based |
| Lentils (1 cup cooked) | 18g | Fiber |
| Whey protein (1 scoop) | 20-25g | Fast-absorbing |

### Protein Distribution

**Spread protein throughout the day for optimal muscle protein synthesis.**

| Meal | Target |
|------|--------|
| Breakfast | 30-40g |
| Lunch | 30-40g |
| Dinner | 30-40g |
| Snack (if needed) | 10-20g |

---

## MEAL TIMING

### Time-Restricted Eating (TRE)

**Eating within a limited window each day.**

| Pattern | Eating Window | Notes |
|---------|---------------|-------|
| 12:12 | 12 hours | Easy starting point |
| 14:10 | 10 hours | Moderate |
| 16:8 | 8 hours | Common, effective |
| 18:6 | 6 hours | More aggressive |

**Benefits:** Insulin sensitivity, autophagy, metabolic health

**See 30_FASTING_AUTOPHAGY for detailed protocols.**

### Eating for Circadian Rhythm

- **Front-load calories** (larger breakfast/lunch, smaller dinner)
- **Stop eating 3 hours before bed**
- **First meal within a few hours of waking** (if not fasting)
- **Morning light** before eating (if practicing TRE)

---

## PRACTICAL IMPLEMENTATION

### Meal Template

**Every meal should include:**
- Protein (palm-sized portion)
- Vegetables (half the plate)
- Healthy fat (thumb-sized portion)
- Complex carbs (fist-sized, if including)

### Simple Swaps

| Instead Of | Try |
|------------|-----|
| White bread | Whole grain or sourdough (or skip) |
| Pasta | Zucchini noodles, spaghetti squash |
| White rice | Cauliflower rice, quinoa |
| Chips | Nuts, vegetable sticks |
| Soda | Sparkling water |
| Juice | Whole fruit |
| Seed oils | Olive oil, avocado oil, butter |

### Reading Labels

**Avoid:**
- Anything you can't pronounce
- High fructose corn syrup
- Partially hydrogenated oils
- Artificial colors/flavors
- Long ingredient lists

**Check:** Serving size, added sugars, fiber, protein

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## DIET SELECTION BY CONDITION

### Condition-Specific Recommendations

| Condition | Primary Approach | Notes |
|-----------|------------------|-------|
| Insulin resistance/T2D | Low-carb or ketogenic | Strong evidence |
| Cardiovascular | Mediterranean | Best evidence |
| Autoimmune | AIP elimination | Then personalize |
| Neurological | Ketogenic or Mediterranean | Depends on condition |
| Gut issues (IBS/SIBO) | Low-FODMAP | During treatment |
| MCAS/Histamine | Low-histamine | Often temporary |
| Weight loss | Low-carb + caloric deficit | Protein priority |
| General optimization | Mediterranean or Whole30 base | Individualize |
| Cancer support | Ketogenic or FMD | With oncology |

### Macronutrient Prescriptions

| Goal | Carbs | Protein | Fat |
|------|-------|---------|-----|
| General health | 30-40% | 25-30% | 30-40% |
| Weight loss | 20-30% | 30-35% | 35-45% |
| Low-carb | 10-20% | 25-30% | 50-65% |
| Ketogenic | 5-10% | 15-20% | 70-80% |
| Athletic performance | 40-50% | 20-25% | 25-35% |

## DETAILED PROTOCOLS

### Insulin Resistance Protocol

**Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1-4)**

*Diet:*
- Reduce carbs to <100g/day
- Eliminate sugar, refined grains
- Protein 30-40g per meal
- Include healthy fats
- Vegetables at every meal

*Timing:*
- Begin TRE (14:10 to start)
- No snacking
- Stop eating 3 hours before bed

**Phase 2: Optimization (Week 5-12)**

*Diet:*
- Further reduce carbs if needed (<50-75g)
- Continue protein priority
- May progress to ketogenic if beneficial

*Timing:*
- Progress TRE to 16:8 if tolerated
- Consider weekly 24h fast

**Phase 3: Maintenance**

- Find sustainable carb level
- Continue TRE
- Monitor glucose, insulin, HbA1c

### Ketogenic Diet Protocol

**Preparation:**
- Educate on macros and food choices
- Stock kitchen appropriately
- Plan for "keto flu"
- Baseline labs

**Implementation:**

*Macros:*
- Carbs: <20-30g net (total - fiber)
- Protein: 1.0-1.5 g/kg body weight
- Fat: Fill remaining calories

*Foods:*
- Meats, fish, eggs
- Above-ground vegetables
- Avocado, olives
- Nuts (limited)
- Cheese (if tolerated)
- Olive oil, coconut oil, butter

*Electrolytes (critical):*
| Electrolyte | Daily Target |
|-------------|--------------|
| Sodium | 3-5g (salt food, broth) |
| Potassium | 3-4g (avocado, greens, lite salt) |
| Magnesium | 300-400mg (supplement) |

**Monitoring:**
- Blood ketones (0.5-3.0 mmol/L target)
- Symptoms and energy
- Weight if goal
- Labs at 4-8 weeks (glucose, insulin, lipids)

**Troubleshooting:**
| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Keto flu | Electrolytes, especially sodium |
| Constipation | Magnesium, fiber from vegetables |
| Bad breath | Temporary, will improve |
| Low energy initially | Normal adaptation, ensure electrolytes |
| Weight stall | Check hidden carbs, calories |

### AIP Implementation Protocol

**Phase 1: Elimination (30-90 days)**

*Strict avoidance:*
- All grains
- All legumes
- All dairy
- Eggs
- Nuts and seeds
- Nightshades
- Alcohol, coffee
- Refined sugars
- NSAIDs

*Focus:*
- Quality meats, organ meats, fish
- Abundant vegetables (non-nightshade)
- Bone broth daily
- Healthy fats (olive oil, coconut, avocado)
- Some fruit

**Phase 2: Reintroduction**

*Order of reintroduction (typically):*
1. Egg yolks
2. Ghee
3. Seeds (individual)
4. Nuts (individual)
5. Whole eggs
6. Dairy (ghee → butter → cream → cheese)
7. Nightshades (individual)
8. Coffee
9. Alcohol (optional)
10. Grains/legumes (some never reintroduce)

*Reintroduction protocol:*
- One food at a time
- Small amount Day 1, larger Day 2-3
- Watch for symptoms 72 hours
- If reaction, wait until clear before next food
- Keep detailed journal

### Elimination Diet Protocol

**Week 1-3: Elimination Phase**

*Remove:*
- Gluten
- Dairy
- Eggs
- Soy
- Corn
- Sugar
- Alcohol
- Caffeine (or limit significantly)
- Processed foods

*Eat:*
- Vegetables (all)
- Fruits
- Quality meats and fish
- Rice (usually tolerated)
- Legumes (if not AIP)
- Nuts and seeds (if not AIP)

**Week 4+: Reintroduction**

*Add back one food every 3-5 days:*
- Eat food 2-3 times on Day 1
- Monitor symptoms Days 1-3
- If no reaction, likely tolerated
- If reaction, remove and note

*Common reintroduction order:*
1. Eggs
2. Dairy (butter → cheese → milk → yogurt)
3. Gluten-free grains (oats, quinoa)
4. Soy
5. Corn
6. Gluten (wheat)

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## DIET BY CONDITION

| Condition | Diet |
|-----------|------|
| Insulin resistance | Low-carb/Keto |
| Cardiovascular | Mediterranean |
| Autoimmune | AIP |
| IBS/SIBO | Low-FODMAP |
| MCAS | Low-histamine |
| Neurological | Ketogenic |

## PROTEIN TARGETS

| Situation | Target |
|-----------|--------|
| General | 0.8-1.0 g/kg |
| Active | 1.2-1.6 g/kg |
| GLP-1/Weight loss | 1.6-2.0 g/kg |
| Per meal | 30-40g |

## KETOGENIC MACROS

- Carbs: <20-30g
- Protein: 1.0-1.5 g/kg
- Fat: 70-80%

**Electrolytes:** Na 3-5g, K 3-4g, Mg 300-400mg

## AIP REMOVES

- Grains, legumes, dairy
- Eggs, nuts, seeds
- Nightshades
- Alcohol, coffee
- Sugar, NSAIDs

## PLATE TEMPLATE

- Half plate: Vegetables
- Quarter plate: Protein
- Quarter plate: Complex carbs (optional)
- Plus: Healthy fat

## TIMING BASICS

- TRE: 16:8 common
- Stop eating 3h before bed
- Protein at every meal
- Front-load calories if possible

---
